zh.Basingstoke and Deane, Hampshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭哈普爾巴辛斯托可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jordan de St Martin於1200年出生在英國多瑟特瓦雷哈姆時，他的父親Jordan de St Martin31歲，母親Alice25歲。他於1219年與Joan Fitzwalter de Neville結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1245年之後在英國哈普爾巴辛斯托可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jordan de St Martin于1200年出生在英国多瑟特瓦雷哈姆时，他的父亲Jordan de St Martin31岁，母亲Alice25岁。他于1219年与Joan Fitzwalter de Neville结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1245年之后在英国哈普爾巴辛斯托可。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a name given to a child of either sex baptized in holy water that was, purportedly at least, brought from the River Jordan, whose Hebrew name, ha-yarden, means ‘flowing down’. It was in this river that Christ was baptized by John the Baptist, and medieval pilgrims to the Holy Land usually tried to bring back a flask of its water with them. The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. It is more popular as a boy's name in Britain and as a girl's name in the United States.

variants.name: Jordin

The modern given name is either a revival of this, or else a transferred use of the surname that was derived from the medieval given name. historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Jordan de St Martin was born in 1200, in Wareham, Dorset, England, his father, Jordan de St Martin, was 31 and his mother, Alice, was 25. He married Joan Fitzwalter de Neville in 1219. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died after 1245, in Basingstoke, Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jordan de St Martin est né en 1200, à Wareham, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Jordan de St Martin, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alice, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Joan Fitzwalter de Neville en 1219. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé après 1245, à Basingstoke, Hampshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jordan de St Martin 1200 in Wareham, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jordan de St Martin 31 und seine Mutter Alice 25. Er hat 1219 Joan Fitzwalter de Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb nach 1245 in Basingstoke, Hampshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jordan de St Martin è nato il nel 1200 nel Wareham, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Jordan de St Martin, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Alice, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Joan Fitzwalter de Neville nel 1219. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il 1245, nel Basingstoke, Hampshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jordan de St Martinが1200年にイギリスドールセトワーエハムで生まれた時，父Jordan de St Martinは31歳，母Aliceは25歳であった。1219年にJoan Fitzwalter de Nevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1245年以後にイギリスハンプシュアイアバシングストクで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jordan de St Martin(이)가 1200년 영국 도르세트 워함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jordan de St Martin(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1219년 Joan Fitzwalter de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1245년 이후 영국 함프실르 바징스토크에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jordan de St Martin nasceu em 1200, em Wareham, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Jordan de St Martin, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Alice, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Fitzwalter de Neville em 1219. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1245, em Basingstoke, Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jordan de St Martin родился в 1200 г. в местности варэам, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Jordan de St Martin было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Alice, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Fitzwalter de Neville в 1219 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер после 1245 г. в местности басингсток, хампшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jordan de St Martin nació en 1200, en Wareham, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Jordan de St Martin, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Alice, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Joan Fitzwalter de Neville en 1219. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió después de 1245, en Basingstoke, Hampshire, Inglaterra.
